parts.answer: A calculation in which: • conversion of actual length of scale into μm (1) • calculation of magnification to two significant figures (1) Example of calculation: 29 to 30 mm = 29 000 to 30 000 (μm) (29 000 ÷ 20) = ×1450 = × 1400 OR 1500 incorrect unit = max 1 please note they may convert the 20 μm into mm or cm e.g. measurement ÷ 0.02 ecf for correct measurement incorrectly converted in working, ÷ 20 and then given to 2 sig fig, e.g. 150 ecf for everything correct with an incorrect measurement shown in working (remember 5 is rounded down OR up) Accept correct standard form to 2 sig fig Correct answer scores full marks (2) these are most common incorrect responses – but please don’t rely on this – always use the MS as your guide 1 mark 2 marks 1.4 x 10 ecf for correct measurement incorrectly converted in working, ÷ 20 and then given to 2 sig fig, e.g. 150 ecf for everything correct with an incorrect measurement shown in working (remember 5 is rounded down OR up) e.g. 45 000÷ 20 = 2 300 OR e.g. 110 ÷ 0.02 = 5500 1.5 x 10 correct answer with incorrect unit 29 000 to 30 000 (μm) 2.9 x 10 to 3.0 x 10 Number Answer Additional guidance Mark • {generative nucleus / it} (divides to) form two (male) {nuclei / gametes} (1) • double fertilisation occurs (1) • one (male) {nucleus / gamete} fuses with {female / egg cell} nucleus to result in {an embryo / a zygote} (1) • one (male) {nucleus / gamete} fuses with (two) polar nuclei to result in endosperm formation (1) ignore sperm cells ignore by meiosis reject generative nucleus reject generative nucleus (3) Number Answer Additional guidance Mark • (loci of) {the genes / flower colour and pollen grain size (genes)} being on same {chromosome / chromatid} (1) accept alleles for genes accept these genes inherited {as single unit / together} ignore they are at the same locus (1) Number Mark B is not correct because correlation coefficient does not analyse observed phenotype frequencies and expected phenotype frequencies. C is not correc
